mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with month-over-month FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising industrial production, capacity utilization improves and corporate margins expand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with directional accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and higher consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns across all market conditions, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4 percent, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of cases where CPIAUCSL monthly growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields negative 20-day returns in 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in industrial production signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining INDPRO during late-cycle expansions fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 45 percent.
